text: • For ACE 1-3, copy and complete the table comparing the smoothie bar segments each family member received. State both unit rates in each comparison.

Emma recorded how many students plan to bring lunch and how many plan to buy lunch when the classes visit the local college.

Complete each ratio comparison statement to describe the data.
a. In Ms. Park's class, for every 3 people bringing lunch, _______ person/people is/are buying lunch.
• b. In Ms. Bloom's class, for every 1 person bringing lunch, _______ person/people is/are buying lunch.
c. In Mr. Henry's class, out of ____ people, 12 are buying lunch.
Shari was learning to measure her heart rate in physical education class. She counted 18 beats in 15 seconds. Set up a rate table to answer the following questions.
a. What is her heart rate in beats per minute?
b. At this rate, how many times will her heart beat in an hour?
a. Gabriela buys songs from a music website. She buys 35 songs for $26.25. What is the price per song?

b. Lola gets a $50 gift card for the music site. She tries to estimate how many songs she can buy with the gift card. Which estimate is the most reasonable? Explain.

i) between 30 and 50 songs

ii) around 70 songs but less than 70

iii) around 70 songs but more than 70

iv) at least 90 songs
While shopping for schools supplies, Emily sees 3 folders for 60¢.
a. What is the unit price for a folder?
b. Write an equation that represents the cost C for N number of folders.
Mateo bought 3 cans of dog food for $3.75.
a. What is the unit price for a can of dog food?
b. Write an equation that represents the cost C for N number of cans of dog food.
At the Central Middle School sixth-grade field day event, there were 348 bottles of water for the 116 students. What is the unit rate of bottles per student?
Cheryl, Rita, and four of their friends go to a movie and share a 48- ounce bag of popcorn equally and three 48-inch licorice laces equally.
a. Write a rati ocomparing the numberof ounces of popcorn to the number of friends.
b. Write a unit rate comparing the number of ounces of popcorn per friend.
c. Write a unit rate comparing the length of licorice lace for each person.
A container of juice mix is 12 ounces. To make the juice, you combine the mix with 36 ounces of water.
a. What is the ratio of juice mix to water?
c. How large of a container will you need to hold the juice?
d. Write an equation that represents the number of ounces of water W needed got M ounces of mix.
Rosco is using a table to plan meals.
a. Complete the rate table for the spaghetti ingredients.
b. What is the unit rate comparing the number of ounces of tomatoes to 1 ounce of spaghetti?
The principal of Washington Middle School is getting a flashlight for every classroom. THere are different types of flashlights. The following ratios come up.

a. List the unit rates.

b. List the ratios that are unit rates. Explain.

c. Write three ratios that are equivalent to 24 batteries for 8 flashlights

Multiple Choice Choose the best score on a math game app.

A. 15 points out of 25

B. 8 points out of 14

C. 25 points out of 45

D. 27 points out of 50

Multiple Choice Choose the best score on a historical trivia app

A. 150 points out of 250

B. 24 points out of 42

C. 75 points out of 135

D. 75 points out of 150

the students at the high school near Washington Middle School decide to hold their own walking backward race. They find that the average high school student can walk 1/3 of a mile in 12 minutes. How many miles would the average high school student cover in 30 minutes? Explain your reasoning.

a. The birds are not similar since the ratio of base length of the larger figure to the base length of the smaller figure is not the same as the ratio of the height of the larger figure to the height of the smaller figure.

b. The figures are similar because the ratio of base length of the larger figure to the base length of the smaller figure is the same as the ratio of the height of the larger figure to the height of the smaller figure. Or, for both width and height the same reduction scale is applied; so, the figures are similar. The scale factor is about 0.7.

c. The figures are not similar because the height of the first figure is reduced by about 56%, while the width is reduced by a smaller percent.

d. The lighthouses are not similar because the height is enlarged but the width is reduced.
For ACE 3-23 through ACE 3-25, decide whether the statement is true or false. Explain your reasoning.

dataSet.name: Students Bringing Lunch vs Buying Lunch
